title: Cross-service checkout degradation with conflicting signals
alert_summary: ALERT: checkout-web and downstream order flows are degraded. Multiple signals are firing, but causal source is unclear.
customer_impact_summary: Customers are experiencing failed or delayed orders, retries, and inconsistent confirmations. The incident spans multiple services and carries high business impact.
root_cause: inventory dependency bottleneck causing cascading payment and checkout latency
misleading_signals: recent payments-api deploy appears correlated but is not root cause

log.message: checkout-web: order creation failures increased sharply over the last 15 minutes
checkout-web: upstream dependency payments-api timed out during order creation
payments-api: dependency inventory-service slow responses causing retry storms
inventory-db: saturation caused by stale invalidation backlog and lock contention
inventory-service: dependency calls blocked waiting on inventory-db response
payments-api: deploy completed near incident start, but no code paths map to inventory read contention

metric.summary: checkout-web latency is materially above baseline
customer-facing errors are increasing on checkout-web
inventory-service queue depth suggests downstream blockage
frontend CPU is elevated but not sufficient to explain the full outage
retry queue growth appears secondary to downstream slowness

notes: Start from customer-visible symptoms, then validate the most likely upstream dependency before resolving.
Do not mark incidents resolved until customer impact is reduced and the suspected root cause is supported by evidence.
If inventory-db saturation is high, restarting the app tier may reduce symptoms briefly without fixing the cause.
When upstream timeouts correlate with high DB usage, inspect dependency health before blaming the nearest service.
Differentiate symptom service from root-cause service by following the failing dependency chain.
A recent deploy can be a red herring. Prefer causal evidence over temporal coincidence.
If one mitigation reduces latency but queue depth and dependency saturation remain high, the root cause is likely unresolved.
